context.description: German philosophical prose critiques speculative collective explanations such as morphogenetic fields and collective memory. It argues that apparent similarities, like those in aircraft design, can arise from underlying laws rather than a collective unconscious, then introduces the hero’s journey and monomyth as the next example.
normalization_notes: Classified as nonfiction book body prose with theoretical discussion.
Book title inferred from source file name.

rows.paragraph_text: The page critiques speculative collective explanations and argues that apparent patterns do not prove a collective unconscious. It uses aerodynamics as an analogy for underlying laws and ends by introducing the hero’s journey, or monomyth, as the next example.

content.free_text: book_page book_body_content theoretical_discussion Bewusstseinsreisen - Version 2, page 71, German philosophical nonfiction. Critique of collective explanations, archetypes, collective unconscious, aerodynamics analogy, and transition to the hero’s journey or monomyth.
